subject: First Aid & Safety
title: How Blood Clots Form
description: A 60-second lesson on How Blood Clots Form.

hook.text: A blood clot forms in under 3 minutes. It's the fastest construction job your body ever does.

buildup.text: When a blood vessel is damaged, platelets rush to the site like emergency workers.

discovery.text: Platelets stick together and release chemicals that activate fibrin—a protein mesh.

twist.text: Fibrin weaves a net over the platelet plug, creating a stable clot that seals the wound.

climax.text: Without this clotting cascade, even a paper cut could be life-threatening.

punchline.text: Your blood builds its own bandage. In under three minutes.

quiz.question: What protein creates a mesh over the platelet plug to stabilize a clot?
